documentation.readme: A python script to automatically coerce a Windows server to authenticate on an arbitrary machine through many methods.
usage_sections: Installation: You can now install it from pypi (latest version is ) with this command:

Quick start: - You want to assess the Remote Procedure Calls listening on a machine to see if they can be leveraged to coerce an authentication? + Use scan mode, example: https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/79218792/204374471-bc5094a3-8539-4df7-842e-faadcaf9c945.mp4 - You want to exploi
Installation: You can now install it from pypi (latest version is ) with this command:

Quick start: - You want to assess the Remote Procedure Calls listening on a machine to see if they can be leveraged to coerce an authentication? + Use scan mode, example: https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/79218792/204374471-bc5094a3-8539-4df7-842e-faadcaf9c945.mp4 - You want to exploi

arguments.name: -d
arguments.description: Windows domain name to authenticate to the machine.

arguments.name: --dc-ip
arguments.description: IP Address of the domain controller. If omitted it will use the domain part (FQDN) specified in the target parameter

arguments.name: --domain
arguments.description: Windows domain name to authenticate to the machine.

arguments.name: --export-json
arguments.description: Export results to specified JSON file.

arguments.name: --export-sqlite
arguments.description: Export results to specified SQLITE3 database file.

arguments.name: --export-xlsx
arguments.description: Export results to specified XLSX file.

arguments.name: -f
arguments.description: File containing a list of IP address or hostname of the target machines

arguments.name: --hashes
arguments.description: NT/LM hashes (LM hash can be empty)

arguments.name: -I
arguments.description: IP address to listen on incoming authentications.

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: Interface to listen on incoming authentications.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Interface to listen on incoming authentications.

arguments.name: --ip-address
arguments.description: IP address to listen on incoming authentications.

arguments.name: -l
arguments.description: IP address or hostname of the listener machine

arguments.name: --listener-ip
arguments.description: IP address or hostname of the listener machine

arguments.name: --log-file
arguments.description: Path for the file to log to (enables logging).

arguments.name: --only-known-exploit-paths
arguments.description: Only test known exploit paths for each functions

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: Password to authenticate to the remote machine. (if omitted, it will be asked unless -no-pass is specified)

arguments.name: --password
arguments.description: Password to authenticate to the remote machine. (if omitted, it will be asked unless -no-pass is specified)

arguments.name: --path-ip
arguments.description: IP address to use when generating exploit paths.

arguments.name: --stop-on-ntlm-auth
arguments.description: Move on to next target on successful NTLM authentication.

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: IP address or hostname of the target machine

arguments.name: --target-ip
arguments.description: IP address or hostname of the target machine

arguments.name: --targets-file
arguments.description: File containing a list of IP address or hostname of the target machines

arguments.name: -u
arguments.description: Username to authenticate to the remote machine.

arguments.name: --username
arguments.description: Username to authenticate to the remote machine.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the post_exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: Windows Support To build a binary for Windows, download the installer.ps1 script from this repository. Run it simply with no arguments to create a binary in the working directory. Use -h or --help for the help menu with .
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A python script to automatically coerce a Windows server to authenticate on an arbitrary machine through many methods.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo python3 -m pip install coercer`. Windows Support To build a binary for Windows, download the installer.ps1 script from this repository. Run it simply with no arguments to create a binary in the working directory. Use -h or --help for the help menu with options.

name: Coercer
summary: Windows Support To build a binary for Windows, download the installer.ps1 script from this repository. Run it simply with no arguments to create a binary in the working directory. Use -h or --help for the help menu with options.
